title: The Real Reason You Cannot Focus: What Attention Residue Is Costing You
excerpt: Every time you switch tasks, part of your attention stays behind. Sophie Leroy named it attention residue. The cost shows up in everything: slower problem-solving, more errors, worse decisions. And most people have no idea they are paying it.

title: Thriving Decades: Why Aging Well Is the Ultimate Performance Goal
excerpt: The longevity industry wants you to live longer. The evidence-based fitness world wants you to train for something more useful: being the person who still hikes at 70, carries their own groceries at 75, and plays on the floor with grandchildren at 80.

title: Why Overthinking Is Your Brain Protecting You — And How to Actually Stop
excerpt: You cannot think your way out of overthinking. The rumination loop is not a logic problem — it is a threat detection system that has gotten too sensitive. Here is what actually interrupts it.

title: Lo-Fi Wellness: The Travel Trend for People Done With Optimizing
excerpt: The wellness travel industry is worth $1.4 trillion and changing course. The next big thing is not a better biohacking clinic. It is a sauna with no WiFi, a sound bath in a barn, and the radical act of doing nothing well.

title: The Wellness Rituals That Work Better in Groups: Why Shared Practice Compounds Results
excerpt: The most effective wellness rituals are not the ones you do alone at 5 AM. They are the ones you do with other people, on a schedule, with some form of accountability built in. Here is why shared practice compounds results in ways solo practice cannot.
